sourceMovie.title: Spun
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Spun works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Filth
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spun worked for you, Filth (2013) is a strong next pick. The two films share crime and drama throughlines and comic timing and sharper banter, even though the stories go in different directions.

recommendations.title: Requiem for a Dream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spun worked for you, Requiem for a Dream (2000) is a strong next pick. The two films share crime and drama throughlines and momentum and clean stakes, even though the stories go in different directions.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spun worked for you, Pineapple Express (2008) is a strong next pick. The two films share crime and comedy throughlines and comic timing and sharper banter, even though the stories go in different directions.

faq.question: What should I watch after Spun?
faq.answer: Try Filth, Requiem for a Dream, Pineapple Express—each shares DNA with Spun while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Spun?
faq.answer: Spun (2003) is best described as Crime, Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Spun worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
